Add ABI regression coverage for that precedence.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-21T09:22:01.082998283Z","created_by":"AmberSignal","updated_at":"2026-04-22T03:57:34.998390505Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T03:57:34.996153142Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

Add focused ABI regression coverage so the forbidden all-ones payload contract is frozen exactly.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"AmberSignal","created_at":"2026-04-21T08:39:08.811494381Z","created_by":"AmberSignal","updated_at":"2026-04-21T08:41:19.937299060Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T08:41:19.934949842Z","close_reason":"Locked down host-parity eventfd_write(valid_fd, UINT64_MAX) behavior: exact EINVAL and new ABI regression coverage; verified locally and via rch.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
-{"id":"bd-8sa88","title":"[MODE-1][HIGH] symbolic correctness: c11threads create/join backend mismatch","description":"[MODE-1][HIGH] symbolic correctness: C11 thread create/join backend contract is internally inconsistent

Validated by current remote gate failure:
- `env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/rch_target_frankenlibc_cod rch exec -- cargo test --workspace --all-targets`
- failing suite: `crates/frankenlibc-abi/tests/c11threads_abi_test.rs`

Concrete mismatch:
- `crates/frankenlibc-abi/src/c11threads_abi.rs:72-110` calls `crate::pthread_abi::pthread_create`, which prefers host `pthread_create` whenever `resolved_thread_create_raw()` is available (`crates/frankenlibc-abi/src/pthread_abi.rs:1991-2015`).
- `crates/frankenlibc-abi/src/c11threads_abi.rs:118-124` then calls `crate::pthread_abi::pthread_join`.
- but `pthread_join` is hard-routed to `native_pthread_join` (`crates/frankenlibc-abi/src/pthread_abi.rs:2019-2021`), which only accepts handles present in `THREAD_HANDLE_REGISTRY` and returns `ESRCH` otherwise (`crates/frankenlibc-abi/src/pthread_abi.rs:1409-1445`).

For host-created threads, the symbolic contract `join(create(f, arg)) = THRD_SUCCESS + propagated return value` is broken: the handle identity model used by `create` is not the one consumed by `join`.

Observed fallout already matches that model break:
- `test_thrd_create_join`
- `test_thrd_join_propagates_zero`
- `test_thrd_join_propagates_negative`
- `test_thrd_join_propagates_large`

Fix direction:
- make C11 thread lifecycle choose one backend consistently across create/join/detach/self/equal, or
- teach `pthread_join` to resolve host-thread handles through the host path instead of assuming every successful create registered a native handle.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-22T08:43:44.824287281Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-22T08:43:44.824287281Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["c11threads","modes","review","symbolic"]}
+{"id":"bd-8sa88","title":"[MODE-1][HIGH] symbolic correctness: c11threads create/join backend mismatch","description":"[MODE-1][HIGH] symbolic correctness: C11 thread create/join backend contract is internally inconsistent

Validated by current remote gate failure:
- `env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/rch_target_frankenlibc_cod rch exec -- cargo test --workspace --all-targets`
- failing suite: `crates/frankenlibc-abi/tests/c11threads_abi_test.rs`

Concrete mismatch:
- `crates/frankenlibc-abi/src/c11threads_abi.rs:72-110` calls `crate::pthread_abi::pthread_create`, which prefers host `pthread_create` whenever `resolved_thread_create_raw()` is available (`crates/frankenlibc-abi/src/pthread_abi.rs:1991-2015`).
- `crates/frankenlibc-abi/src/c11threads_abi.rs:118-124` then calls `crate::pthread_abi::pthread_join`.
- but `pthread_join` is hard-routed to `native_pthread_join` (`crates/frankenlibc-abi/src/pthread_abi.rs:2019-2021`), which only accepts handles present in `THREAD_HANDLE_REGISTRY` and returns `ESRCH` otherwise (`crates/frankenlibc-abi/src/pthread_abi.rs:1409-1445`).

For host-created threads, the symbolic contract `join(create(f, arg)) = THRD_SUCCESS + propagated return value` is broken: the handle identity model used by `create` is not the one consumed by `join`.

Observed fallout already matches that model break:
- `test_thrd_create_join`
- `test_thrd_join_propagates_zero`
- `test_thrd_join_propagates_negative`
- `test_thrd_join_propagates_large`

Fix direction:
- make C11 thread lifecycle choose one backend consistently across create/join/detach/self/equal, or
- teach `pthread_join` to resolve host-thread handles through the host path instead of assuming every successful create registered a native handle.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-22T08:43:44.824287281Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-22T09:28:24.610214936Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T09:28:24.608033863Z","close_reason":"Duplicate of bd-21ypi / bd-jqy64 — same c11threads create/join backend mismatch. Fix landed in commit 138c07c3 (pthread_join now delegates to host for non-managed handles). c11threads_abi_test suite is green (36/0). bd-jqy64 remains in_progress under cod's ownership.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["c11threads","modes","review","symbolic"]}
 {"id":"bd-8sgm6","title":"[MODE-3][MEDIUM] ABI compatibility: printf executor hardcodes LP64 widths","description":"[MODE-3][MEDIUM] ABI compatibility: printf conformance executor hardcodes LP64 vararg widths

Recent printf executor work widened promotion and dispatch handling, but the ABI model is still host-width-specific:
- `crates/frankenlibc_conformance/src/lib.rs:2492-2496` stores long-like integers as `PrintfArg::Long(c_longlong)` and `PrintfArg::Ulong(c_ulonglong)`.
- `crates/frankenlibc_conformance/src/lib.rs:2502-2585` treats `%l`, `%j`, `%z`, `%t`, and `%q` as a single `long_like` bucket and rewrites integer args into those 64-bit slots.

That matches the current LP64 host, but it is not an ABI-stable model for width-sensitive formats such as `%ld`, `%zd`, and `%td`. On non-LP64 targets the executor can mis-marshal arguments and report false conformance parity.

This is an ABI review finding rather than a current Linux x86_64 failure, but it directly affects whether the conformance harness can be trusted when the matrix expands past today's host ABI.

Fix direction:
- split `long`, `long long`, `size_t`, `ptrdiff_t`, and `intmax_t` by actual target width
- source widths from explicit target metadata instead of one 64-bit promotion bucket.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-22T08:46:05.341784917Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-22T08:46:05.341784917Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["abi","modes","printf","review"]}
 {"id":"bd-8sho","title":"bd-1j4 subtask: Hard-parts docs/parity/support-matrix truth reconciliation","description":"Background:
- Hard-parts completion claims require synchronized docs/support matrix/feature parity narratives.

Goal:
- Reconcile hard-parts implementation reality with user-facing claims and machine-generated reports.

Deliverables:
1) Hard-parts status truth table across README/FEATURE_PARITY/support_matrix/reality report.
2) Drift guard updates for hard-parts claim consistency.
3) Explicit “deferred vs implemented” rationale for each hard-parts subsystem.

Acceptance Criteria:
- No contradictory hard-parts claims across docs/artifacts.
- Drift gates catch future inconsistency automatically.

Verification & Logging:
- Drift guard tests and fixtures.
- Structured logs for reconciliation checks and mismatches.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"RedMaple","created_at":"2026-02-12T15:03:33.576518493Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-13T08:52:20.345066285Z","closed_at":"2026-02-13T08:52:20.345031921Z","close_reason":"Completed hard-parts truth table reconciliation across README/FEATURE_PARITY/support_matrix/reality_report with new drift guard script, harness test, and CI wiring.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["critique","docs","drift","hard-parts","verification"],"comments":[{"id":1063,"issue_id":"bd-8sho","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"RedMaple claiming bd-8sho and starting hard-parts truth reconciliation across README/FEATURE_PARITY/support_matrix/reality_report.\n\nPlanned deliverables:\n1)  artifact with per-subsystem implemented vs deferred rationale.\n2)  drift guard script.\n3)  guard test.\n4) doc updates in  and  to include explicit hard-parts truth lines tied to the artifact.","created_at":"2026-02-13T08:48:02Z"},{"id":1064,"issue_id":"bd-8sho","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Progress update (RedMaple): completed hard-parts docs/parity/support/reality truth reconciliation with drift guard wiring.\n\nImplemented:\n- New canonical artifact: tests/conformance/hard_parts_truth_table.v1.json\n  - per-subsystem status + implemented/deferred scope rationale for startup/threading/resolver/nss/locale/iconv\n  - support_matrix expectations per subsystem\n  - reality_report snapshot binding + contradiction summary\n- New drift guard: scripts/check_hard_parts_truth.sh\n  - validates artifact schema, README + FEATURE_PARITY hard-parts truth lines, support_matrix expectations, and reality_report snapshot alignment\n  - emits structured JSON event with trace_id/mode/api_family/outcome/artifact refs\n- New harness test: crates/frankenlibc-harness/tests/hard_parts_truth_test.rs\n  - artifact schema validation\n  - guard script execution gate\n- Docs reconciled:\n  - README.md hard-parts truth section added\n  - FEATURE_PARITY.md hard-parts truth section added\n- CI wiring:\n  - scripts/ci.sh now runs scripts/check_hard_parts_truth.sh\n\nVerification executed (PASS):\n- scripts/check_hard_parts_truth.sh\n- CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/data/tmp/cargo-target-codex-harness cargo test -p frankenlibc-harness --test hard_parts_truth_test\n- CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/data/tmp/cargo-target-codex-harness cargo test -p frankenlibc-harness --test parity_report_drift_test\n\nNote:\n- /tmp is full on this host; verification used CARGO_TARGET_DIR under /data/tmp for deterministic local runs.","created_at":"2026-02-13T08:52:17Z"}]}

 {"id":"bd-q0ss","title":"fix: gethostbyname2_r preserves h_errno on ERANGE","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-20T21:10:26.698091531Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-20T21:14:46.681039628Z","closed_at":"2026-04-20T21:14:46.679031413Z","close_reason":"Matched host ERANGE/h_errno contract for IPv6 gethostbyname2_r","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
-{"id":"bd-q5uvh","title":"[MODE-2][HIGH] concurrency safety: c11threads join fails to provide completion ordering","description":"[MODE-2][HIGH] concurrency safety: failed C11 join destroys the only completion fence for mutex and condvar tests

This is the concurrency consequence of the same backend split, and it is independently dangerous because it removes the intended happens-before edge.

Validated by the current workspace gate failure:
- `env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/rch_target_frankenlibc_cod rch exec -- cargo test --workspace --all-targets`

Evidence in tests:
- `crates/frankenlibc-abi/tests/c11threads_abi_test.rs:312-340` expects four worker threads to complete before reading `MTX_COUNTER`, but uses `thrd_join` as the only completion barrier.
- `crates/frankenlibc-abi/tests/c11threads_abi_test.rs:182-231` expects `cnd_wait` wakeup accounting to be stable only after `thrd_join` returns.

Because `thrd_join` currently fails for host-created threads, the test thread often reads shared state before the worker has actually terminated. That explains the observed undercount and lost-wakeup symptoms:
- mutex contention counter `304` instead of `400`
- condition-variable wake count `0` or `1` instead of the expected completed waiter count

This is more than a test issue: any caller relying on C11 join for publication and completion ordering can observe stale shared state or partially-completed critical sections.

Fix direction:
- restore a real join completion path for host-created C11 threads
- then keep the failing mutex and condvar tests as regression gates because they are exposing a real synchronization contract breach.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-22T08:45:43.085212253Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-22T08:45:43.085212253Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["c11threads","concurrency","modes","review"]}
+{"id":"bd-q5uvh","title":"[MODE-2][HIGH] concurrency safety: c11threads join fails to provide completion ordering","description":"[MODE-2][HIGH] concurrency safety: failed C11 join destroys the only completion fence for mutex and condvar tests

This is the concurrency consequence of the same backend split, and it is independently dangerous because it removes the intended happens-before edge.

Validated by the current workspace gate failure:
- `env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/rch_target_frankenlibc_cod rch exec -- cargo test --workspace --all-targets`

Evidence in tests:
- `crates/frankenlibc-abi/tests/c11threads_abi_test.rs:312-340` expects four worker threads to complete before reading `MTX_COUNTER`, but uses `thrd_join` as the only completion barrier.
- `crates/frankenlibc-abi/tests/c11threads_abi_test.rs:182-231` expects `cnd_wait` wakeup accounting to be stable only after `thrd_join` returns.

Because `thrd_join` currently fails for host-created threads, the test thread often reads shared state before the worker has actually terminated. That explains the observed undercount and lost-wakeup symptoms:
- mutex contention counter `304` instead of `400`
- condition-variable wake count `0` or `1` instead of the expected completed waiter count

This is more than a test issue: any caller relying on C11 join for publication and completion ordering can observe stale shared state or partially-completed critical sections.

Fix direction:
- restore a real join completion path for host-created C11 threads
- then keep the failing mutex and condvar tests as regression gates because they are exposing a real synchronization contract breach.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-22T08:45:43.085212253Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-22T09:28:38.129225764Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T09:28:38.125880575Z","close_reason":"Same root cause as bd-21ypi/bd-8sa88/bd-jqy64 — fixed in commit 138c07c3. The 'mutex count 304 vs 400' and 'cnd wake 0/1 vs expected' symptoms flowed from thrd_join failing for host-created threads; with pthread_join now delegating to the host backend, test_mtx_multi_threaded_contention, test_cnd_wait_signal, test_cnd_broadcast_wakes_all all pass (36/0 in c11threads_abi_test). The synchronization contract breach is resolved at the join boundary.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["c11threads","concurrency","modes","review"]}
